title: The Spatial Thinking Ability Test (STAT-24):Classroom administration, scoring, and data management
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"x15be","text":"This protocol standardizes the classroom administration, scoring, and data management of the Spatial Thinking Ability Test (STAT-24), a category-balanced instrument for secondary geography education. It details preparation (ethics, consent, anonymized IDs), delivery in paper, a verbatim instruction script, 45-minute timing (60 minutes with approved accommodations), incident logging, and secure collection of materials. Scoring converts A–E responses to 1–5, with quality control via double entry or automated checks, and versioned separation of raw versus derived datasets. Data management emphasizes privacy (no PII in analysis files, encrypted ID crosswalk) and documentation (README, data dictionary, checksums). Optional analyses outline CTT, CFA (WLSMV), IRT (NRM), and DIF procedures to support validity and fairness evidence. Expected outputs include an anonymized UTF-8 CSV with item responses and category subscores suitable for research use and formative feedback. This protocol was applied in 2024–2025 with N=923 Grade-11 students across 7 schools in Mongolia.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"hxxiv","text":"Public package includes administration workflow, templates, and a sampler booklet to preserve test security; the full STAT-24 booklet and answer key can be provided on reasonable request for non-high-stakes research or instructional use.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7vlmp","text":"Ethics \u0026 privacy. Obtain IRB/ethics approval; collect and securely store consent. Never include PII in analysis files; keep the ID crosswalk separate and access-restricted.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"85lnr","text":"Standardization. Use the provided instruction script verbatim; keep environmental conditions consistent (timing, aids, seating).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aka1k","text":"Security. Protect test content; store booklets separately from answer sheets; control digital access (session codes, device checks).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4r9lq","text":"Contingencies. Maintain a paper fallback for digital delivery; log all incidents and deviations.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"21p0f","text":"Data integrity. Use UTF-8 CSV; validate ranges and missingness; version all files; separate raw vs. derived datasets.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"lj3a","text":"Accommodations. Provide extended time only when pre-approved; document accommodations in the incident log (no PII).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cm2dj","text":"Interpretation caution. Category-level decisions should favor class-level patterns and repeated measures over high-stakes, single-sitting inferences.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"q0lxl","text":"Before you begin","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8s989","text":"Ethics \u0026 approvals: IRB/ethics approval obtained; parental and/or student consent collected and archived.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6p00p","text":"Access \u0026 roles: Assign a coordinator (overall), invigilators (in-room), and a data manager (post-session).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9mfsg","text":"Delivery mode: Choose Paper (booklet + answer sheet) or Digital (e.g., Exam.net). Prepare backups for outages.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2g8ol","text":"Anonymization plan: Decide on student ID schema, store crosswalk securely, and keep separate from research data.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8olro","text":"Answer key \u0026 templates: Verify the STAT-24 answer key; prepare scoring sheet and CSV template.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"15vo9","text":"Estimated prep time: 45–90 min.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"qk1xb","text":"No explicit warnings or safety cautions on these pages.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"ekh8i","text":"Documents: STAT-24 booklet (PDF/print), answer sheet template, standardized instruction script, consent forms, IRB letter.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3vtne","text":"Software: R (packages: lavaan, mirt, difr, GDINA ), Excel Sheets","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"60uqp","text":"Hardware: Printer/scanner","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Confirm IRB/ethics approval and collect required consent (parental). Create anonymized student ID list and secure the ID↔name crosswalk separately. Verify answer key and scoring sheet; prepare CSV template (UTF-8). Brief invigilators on standardized script, timing (45 min; 60 min with approved accommodations), and incident logging.

Arrange seating to minimize collaboration; display a visible timer. Distribute anonymized IDs; verify each student records the correct ID on the answer sheet/form. Perform a quick 5-student spot-check for correct ID entry.
Invigilators circulate quietly; no content assistance. Record incidents (late arrivals, suspected collaboration, device failure)

Collect all booklets and answer sheets (paper). Reconcile counts with the roster; investigate discrepancies and document resolutions. Store test booklets separately from answer sheets to protect item security.

Encode responses A–E → 1–5 (blank = NA/0 as defined in your codebook). Enter data into CSV (UTF-8). Columns: ID, Item1…Item24, optional non-identifying demographics. Apply official STAT-24 answer key; compute total and category subscores. Save raw (verbatim) and scored/derived datasets as separate versioned files.

Remove PII (names, class, school codes) from all analysis files. Store the ID crosswalk in a restricted, encrypted location; keep separate from analysis data. Create/update a README with file versions, dates, checksums, and responsible person. For sharing, provide only anonymized data and documentation.

CTT: item difficulty (target ~0.20–0.80), discrimination, item-total correlations. CFA (WLSMV): confirm category structure; report fit indices and factor loadings. IRT (NRM): inspect option information and discrimination; flag uninformative options. Fairness: DIF checks (e.g., gender, location) and report flagged items. Summarize category-level results and note limitations (precision, classification reliability).
creator: tsendsuren tserendolgor
